title: Willdenowia ecklonii
taxonomy: Willdenowia ecklonii is classified as a species of fern, specifically within the genus Willdenowia. This genus belongs to the family Restionaceae. The family Restionaceae is further classified under the order Restionales, which is part of the class Liliopsida. The Liliopsida is a subclass of the angiosperm division (Magnoliophyta) in the kingdom Plantae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and anatomical characteristics.
uses: Willdenowia ecklonii is traditionally used in some African cultures for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of various health issues. It has been used to treat fever, coughs, and respiratory problems. The plant's leaves and stems are often crushed and mixed with water to create a medicinal tea.
